steps.title: Identify the property at issue
steps.content: The passage states that silver exhibits “high electrical conductivity” immediately before the blank. This is the one stated fact that will drive the researchers' hypothesis.

steps.title: Connect the property to the result
steps.content:
Given that silver's high electrical conductivity was added and power output more than doubled, the researchers would logically hypothesize that this conductivity provides a solution to the identified problem: the inefficient redox reactions. Choice D states this directly—conductivity allows electrons to bypass those reactions and transfer straight to the electrode.
A incorrectly focuses on bacterial metabolism rather than electron transfer efficiency, B contradicts the experiment by implying silver's addition is irrelevant, and C reverses the logic by suggesting silver catalyzes the problematic reactions.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
